questions.answer:
(i) The class name is Hello.
(ii) The file name should be Hello.java.
(iii) The main method starts execution.
(iv) The line System.out.println("Good morning"); prints the output.
(v) A semicolon ends the statement.

(i) The class name is Marks.
(ii) The variable is marks.
(iii) The data type is int.
(iv) The value assigned is 95.
(v) The statement System.out.println(marks); displays the value.

(i) The class name is Demo.
(ii) The command used is System.out.print().
(iii) No, the output does not move to the next line automatically.
(iv) The statement is written inside the main method.
(v) The semicolon marks the end of the statement.

(i) The class name is Price.
(ii) The data type used is double.
(iii) It is suitable because it stores decimal values.
(iv) The value stored in price is 45.75.
(v) The statement System.out.println(price); shows the output.

(i) The two variables are a and b.
(ii) The operator used is +.
(iii) The operands are a and b.
(iv) The value produced is 900.
(v) a + b is an expression.

(i) The class name is RemainderDemo.
(ii) The operator used is %.
(iii) It gives the remainder after division.
(iv) The value stored in rem is 2.
(v) The statement System.out.println(rem); displays rem.
